content:
Hello team,
There is a requirement of mine where userland code makes an ioctl call. Is it currently supported? If yes, a working example will be helpful.

Thank you.
Sarbojit

You can found a usage of IOCTL in this sample: https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/blob/rust/samples/rust/rust_semaphore.rs

Some macro like _IOR / _IOW / _IOC are not present in rust, so you may need to write "magic constants" to handle commands.
You can use a C header file to define the commands. Binder does that.
Can I have an example for this ? (Actually I try to create a chardev driver which will need IOCTL, but I wrote magic numbers in rust, it will be nice if I can just use the C macros)
The binder header file is shared with the C implementation, but the same would work even if you don't use it from C.
Hoo, so I just have to define constants in header and include them in the binding generation, very smart ! Thanks !
On top of that, some const fns will likely land soon (https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20230224-rust-ioctl-v1-1-5142d365a934@asahilina.net/ -- there will be a v2 though)
